fear: Being identified as the intermediary in a state where harboring is a crime but so, increasingly, is being the man who set the law on a group that includes children — Ohio juries are unpredictable.
name: Arden Gill
voice: Smooth, commercial, practiced in the language of reasonable men doing reasonable business — never uses the word slave, says 'property' or 'the owner's interest.'
secrets: Jerome Lattimore, his source inside the network, does not know that Gill has given his name to the slave owner as a bonus piece of intelligence — Lattimore himself is now at risk of being sold south by the very men he is helping.
Gill carries a copy of the bounty description in his coat pocket, already signed by the owner's agent.
knowledge: He knows the name of the slave owner who posted the original bounty and the name of the professional catcher he has hired — a man named Dodd who is currently riding north with Deputy Crane.
He knows the source above Silas Pratt — a free Black man named Jerome Lattimore who was himself a passenger three years ago and who has been feeding information to Gill for money ever since.
He does not know about Thomas Cole's interference with the wire and believes the red fire dispatch has gone through cleanly.
publicFace: A traveling agent for a Cincinnati dry goods wholesaler — plausible, well-dressed, present in Sandusky for legitimate commercial reasons at least three times a year.
represents: REQUIRES FACT-CHECK — verify whether this is a documented individual from colonial Boston, 1774-1776
trustLogic: Performs as a neutral commercial party; opens slightly to anyone who seems sympathetic to the owner's legal rights under the 1850 Act. Closes hard at any mention of Silas Pratt by name.
privateGoal: Ensure the catchers intercept Elias Cole's group tonight, collect the broker's fee from the bounty, and leave Sandusky before anyone connects him to Silas Pratt or the red fire dispatch.

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Boards the next southbound train with his commercial luggage, entirely within his rights as a traveling man.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Does not flee physically — calls for the law, loudly, claiming he is being robbed or harassed.
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Expresses polite confusion, produces his commercial credentials, suggests the questioner has mistaken him for someone else.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not name Jerome Lattimore under any pressure — Lattimore is his only reliable inside source and Gill intends to use him again.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the bounty document in his coat pocket is demanded by someone with legal authority, or if Dodd is arrested.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Invokes the Fugitive Slave Act directly and by section number, suggests that interference with lawful recovery of property is a federal offense.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Produces the bounty document and the owner's legal authorization and claims he is simply a courier for a lawful federal process.
